applicable_version: Windows Server 2008 and later where the audit subcategory is enabled
name: A member was added to a security-enabled universal group
definition: Windows Security Event ID 4756 records addition of a member to a security-enabled universal group.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents 4756 as the universal-group counterpart to 4732, with the same fields and recommendations except group type.

key_fields.explanation: The principal added to the universal security group.

key_fields.field: Group Name / Group Domain / Group Security ID
key_fields.explanation: The affected universal group, which can be granted access in any trusting domain.
Newer event versions may include membership expiration time; privileges captures special rights involved in the change.

false_positives: Approved cross-domain access provisioning can add users to universal groups.
Identity governance platforms may add temporary group memberships with expiration time.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Account Manipulation: Additional Local or Domain Groups

detection_notes: Use threshold=1 for additions to privileged universal groups or groups granting cross-domain access, especially when Expiration time is blank or unexpectedly long. Microsoft documents 4756 as the universal-group equivalent of 4732; adding a Member Security ID to a universal security group can extend access across trusting domains and supports T1098.007 Additional Local or Domain Groups. Correlate Subject Logon ID with 4624/4688 and validate the provisioning request.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4756
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, MemberName, GroupName, GroupDomain
spl_snippet: index=wineventlog EventCode=4756 | table _time, host, Account_Name, Member_Account_Name, Group_Name, Group_Domain
sample_log:
EventID: 4756
Subject Account Name: admin.ops
Subject Logon ID: 0x27a79
Member Security ID: S-1-5-21-111-222-333-1109
Member Account Name: CN=svc-sync,CN=Users,DC=corp,DC=local
Group Name: Forest-App-Admins
Group Domain: CORP
Expiration time: -

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4756
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4756
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Map raw XML fields to the documented fields.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4756
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4756
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Add the documented event-specific conditions after field validation.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Security Event ID 4756
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested.
Inspect raw fields because parsed names vary.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 4756
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 4756
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4756
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.

value_references.meaning: The principal added to the universal security group.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret MemberSid with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: A blank or dash value means no temporary membership expiry is represented.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret ExpirationTime with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4756 mean?
faqs.answer: A member was added to a security-enabled universal group.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 4756 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Normal provisioning and role management generate it.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 4756 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Assess group privilege and cross-domain reach, validate expiration and approval, and correlate the subject logon with process activity.

sources.title: 4756: A member was added to a security-enabled universal group
Account Manipulation: Additional Local or Domain Groups (T1098.007)
